item.name: Quickstart
item.description:
In this guide we will walk you through all necessary steps to start accessing data from practice.

1.Authorized practices:

This API will give you the list of practices from which you can access data

GET https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/authorized_practices HTTP/1.1
Host: api.sikkasoft.com
App-Id: <application id>
App-Key: <application key>
  • Collect 'office_id' and 'secret_key'

2.POST request_key API call:
API to get Request Key for practices:

In response of ‘authorized_practices’ API you have ‘office_id’ and ‘secret_key’. Use it in POST request_key API

POST https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key
Body
{
"grant_type":"request_key",
"office_id":"",
"secret_key":""
"app_id":"app_id",
"app_key":"app_key"
}

Response

{
"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key_info?request_key=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
"request_key": "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
"start_time": "2020-09-23T04:00:28",
"end_time": "2020-09-24T04:00:28",
"expires_in": "85603 second(s)",
"issued_to": "D20006",
"status": "active",
"request_count": "1",
"scope": "start,end,appointment_statuses",
"domain": "Dental",
"refresh_key":"xxxx"
}
  • Collect 'request_key'

3.Patients API call:
API to get the list of patients

https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients
Host: api.sikkasoft.com 
request-key:  <request_key>

More information about a particular endpoints are in API’s documentation , which includes query parameters the API will accept.

item.name: Authorization
Generate request_key
item.description:
POST https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key
Header: Content-Type:applicaion/json
Body:
{
"grant_type":"request_key/refresh_key",[required]
"office_id":"",[required if grant_type=request_key.Get it from authorized_practices API]
"secret_key":"",[required if grant_type=request_key.Get it from authorized_practices API]
"refresh_key":"",[required if grant_type=refresh_key]
"app_id":"app_id",[required]
"app_key":"app_key"[required]
}

✅ "grant_type" must be either "request_key" or "refresh_key"

Request for create new request_key
POST https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key
Header: Content-Type:applicaion/json
Body:
{
"grant_type":"request_key",
"office_id":"office_id",
"secret_key":"secret_key",
"app_id":"app_id",
"app_key":"app_key"
}

Response: In response you will get 'request_key' along with other details like request end time , expires_in , for office_id that request_key issued to , scope etc.

{
    "href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key_info?request_key=4d74aaaxxxx22b",
    "request_key": "4d74aaaxxxx22b",
    "start_time": "2020-09-23T04:00:28",
    "end_time": "2020-09-24T04:00:28",
    "expires_in": "85603 second(s)",
    "issued_to": "D20006",
    "status": "active",
    "request_count": "1",
    "scope": "start,end,appointment_statuses,appointments,claims,clinical_conditions,contact_details,data_check,dental_patients,drugs,employee,fee_schedules,guarantors,insurance_accounts,insurance_accounts_receivables,insurance_companies,insurance_company_details,insurance_estimate,insurance_plan_coverage,lab_panels,lab_results,medical_notes,patient_charts,patient_conditions,patient_statuses,patients,payment_plans,payment_types,practices,procedure_codes,providers,request_keys,transactions,treatment_plan_statuses,treatment_plans,applications,authorized_practices,pms_list,request_keys,request_keys_details,POST practice_query",
    "domain": "Dental",
"refresh_key":"xxxxxxxxx"
}

item.name: Refresh token
item.description:
Request_keys expire 24 hours after they are issued. The refresh token can be used to make a request for a new request_key, similar to the initial access token exchange. Refresh tokens don’t expire until you delete refresh token.

Request

POST https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key
Header: Content-Type:applicaion/json
Body:
{
"grant_type":"refresh_key",
"app_id":"app_id",
"app_key":"app_key"
}

Response:

{
    "href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key_info?request_key=4d74aaaxxxx22b",
    "request_key": "4d74aaaxxxx22b",
    "start_time": "2020-09-23T04:00:28",
    "end_time": "2020-09-24T04:00:28",
    "expires_in": "85603 second(s)",
    "issued_to": "D20006",
    "status": "active",
    "request_count": "1",
    "scope": "start,end,appointment_statuses,appointments,claims,clinical_conditions,contact_details,data_check,dental_patients,drugs,employee,fee_schedules,guarantors,insurance_accounts,insurance_accounts_receivables,insurance_companies,insurance_company_details,insurance_estimate,insurance_plan_coverage,lab_panels,lab_results,medical_notes,patient_charts,patient_conditions,patient_statuses,patients,payment_plans,payment_types,practices,procedure_codes,providers,request_keys,transactions,treatment_plan_statuses,treatment_plans,applications,authorized_practices,pms_list,request_keys,request_keys_details,POST practice_query",
    "domain": "Dental",
"refresh_key":"xxxxxxxxx"
}

item.name: Revoking tokens
item.description:
If you want to delete request_key or refresh_key

Delete request_key

Delete https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key
Header:  
Content-Type:applicaion/json  
Request-Key:"xxxxxxx"

Response:

{
    "http_code": "200",
    "http_code_desc": "OK",
    "error_code": "API0002",
    "short_message": "OK",
    "long_message": "Request Session terminated successfully.",
    "more_information": ""
}

item.name: Delete refresh_key
item.description:
Delete https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/refresh_key
Header:  
Content-Type:applicaion/json  
Refresh-Key:""  
App-Id:"`application-id>`"  
App-Key:"`application secret key`"

Response:

{
    "http_code": "200",
    "http_code_desc": "OK",
    "error_code": "API0002",
    "short_message": "OK",
    "long_message": "Request Session terminated successfully.",
    "more_information": ""
}

request.description:
Accepts an request key and returns information about that request key including which office id was it issued to, the intended target of the request key, the scopes the user consented to, the remaining lifetime of the request key.

Parameters

request_key required

Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

header.value: default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' * data: blob:; script-src * 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' ; img-src * data: blob:; font-src * 'unsafe-inline' data: blob:;

    "href": "http://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/request_key_info?request_key=########",

item.name: API Field Filtering
item.description:
The default endpoints of the Sikka REST API are designed to be sensible defaults in terms of what data is returned.

Fields parameter provides a single way for modifying all responses for an object. For example, in patients API there are default fields but if your application needs more information, fields parameter allows you to get more fields.

Important Note about Field Filtering

The API exposes only default fields on responses. There are some information you will be needed but if it is not included in response then you can get that information using 'fields' parameter. While it's tempting to modify or add fields in responses, this will cause problems with API clients that expect standard responses. This includes things like mobile clients or third party tools.

You may only need a small amount of data, but it's important to keep in mind that the API is about exposing an interface to all clients, not just the feature you're working on.

What fields parameter does ?
Every Sikka API allows to return only default fields. Other fields will not be display in API response. To get more information or fields in response user can use 'fields' parameter.

How to Use Fields Parameter?

Sikka API accepts fields as parameters:

In fields parameters users needs to specify which fields API should return in response.

This means that if you specify

https//api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients?fields=firstname,lastname, cell

It will return default fields & first name , last name & cell. It will skip / hide other fields.

Examples

URL : https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices

Response :

{
"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1",
"practice_id": "1",
"name": "Second Site Location",
"address_line1": "12345 Any Street",
"address_line2": "",
"city": "Mt. Vernon",
"state": "IL",
"zipcode": "62864",
"phone": "",
"country": "",
"email": "",
"providers": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/providers/"},
"patients": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/patients/"},
"guarantors": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/guarantors/"},
"appointments": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/appointments/"},
"transactions": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/transactions/"}
}

If user wants to see “website” in response with other fields

URL : https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices?fields=website

Response :

{
"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1",
"practice_id": "1",
"name": "Second Site Location",
"address_line1": "12345 Any Street",
"address_line2": "",
"city": "Mt. Vernon",
"state": "IL",
"zipcode": "62864",
"phone": "",
"country": "",
"email": "",
"website":"",
"providers": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/providers/"},
"patients": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/patients/"},
"guarantors": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/guarantors/"},
"appointments": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/appointments/"},
"transactions": {"href": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practices/1/transactions/"}
}

How to get the list of all fields?

By default each api returns default fields with necessary information. If you want to get all fields and want to check what other fields are there in api response , use 'get_all' value for 'fields' parameter.
example :
get the list of all fields of 'patients' api

URL : https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients

In response you will get all fields of 'patients' api

How to get only fields you want & skip default fields ?

By default each api returns default fields along with fields you want in fields parameters. But in a few cases the user wants only the required data without default fields.

In that case users can use lightweight parameter. lightweight parameter is boolean

How to Use lightweight Parameter?

Set lightweight = true & in fields parameters users need to specify which fields API should return in response.

lightweight=true parameter is not recommended to use in practice_events api

This means that if you specify

https//api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients?lightweight=true&fields=firstname,lastname,cell

It will return first name , last name & cell only.

Response :

"limit":"1"
"total_count":"27598"
"execution_time":"157"
"pagination":{
"first":"https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients?offset=0&limit=1"
"previous":""
"current":"https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients?offset=0&limit=1"
"next":"https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients?offset=1&limit=1"
"last":"https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients?offset=27597&limit=1"
}
"items":[
{
"firstname":"Hayden"
"lastname":"Johnson"
"cell":"xxx-xxx-xxxx"
}
]
}

item.name: Pagination
item.description:
Maximum 5000 records fetch available as per Sikka API license. Requests for collections can return between 0 and 5000 results, controlled using the limit and offset query parameters. All end points are limited to 50 results by default.

GET /v4/patients?offset=0&limit=499 Return rows from 0 to 499

The offset should be the page offset (not the item offset). Also, limit will denote page size, along with limit of rows returned.

offset = 0 and limit = 500 will return the 1st 500 rows
offset = 1 and limit = 500 will return rows 501 to 1000
offset = 2 and limit = 500 will return rows 1001 to 1500
offset = 3 and limit = 500 will return rows 1501 to 2000

"pagination" in response:

We have added pagination links in API response. So user can automate the API invocation with hrefs of different pagination links.

[{
"offset": "0",
"limit": "2",
"total_count": "6",
"execution_time": "71",
"pagination": {
"first": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/{api_name}?offset=0&limit=2",
"previous": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/{api_name}?offset=0&limit=2",
"current": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/{api_name}?offset=1&limit=2",
"next": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/{api_name}?offset=2&limit=2",
"last": "https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/{api_name}?offset=2&limit=2"
},
"items": [{
....
....
....
}]
}]

When retrieving data from an API with pagination, it's rare to encounter errors while navigating through pages, but if you encounter errors then to handle these errors and retry the same pagination request without starting from scratch, you can follow these steps:

Set up an error handling mechanism: Before making the API request, ensure that you have a way to capture and handle errors. This could involve using try-catch blocks or implementing error handlers provided by the programming language or framework you are using.

Make the initial pagination request: Send the initial request to retrieve the first page of data from the API. Include the necessary parameters for pagination, such as the page number and page size.

Process the response: Once you receive the response from the API, check if it contains the expected data. If the response is successful, proceed with your data processing logic. If the response indicates an error, handle it appropriately based on the error information provided by the API.

Retry on error: If an error occurs while navigating through the pages, you can retry the same pagination request by sending the request again with the same parameters. However, it's important to introduce some mechanism to avoid infinite retries in case of persistent errors. You can set a maximum number of retries or implement an exponential backoff strategy, where you increase the wait time between retries.

Retain state between retries: To retain the same state during retries, you need to store the necessary information from the initial request. This includes the pagination parameters (e.g., page number, page size) and any other relevant context. You can store this information in variables or data structures that persist across retry attempts, such as global variables or session variables.

Retry with updated parameters: If you encounter an error while retrying the pagination request, adjust the necessary parameters before sending the request again. For example, you might need to update the page number to the next page or adjust other parameters based on the error response or the specific requirements of the API.

Handle different types of errors: API errors can vary, so it's essential to handle them accordingly. Some errors might indicate a temporary issue, such as a server overload or a connection problem. In such cases, retrying the request after a brief delay might be sufficient. Other errors could indicate a permanent issue, such as an invalid request or authentication failure. Handle these errors appropriately and consider logging them for further investigation if needed.

By following these steps, you can handle errors while navigating through API pages and retry the pagination request while retaining the same state, allowing you to recover from errors and continue fetching the data you need.

item.name: Rate Limiting
item.description:
What is rate limiting?

Most APIs are subject to a limit on how many calls can be made per second (or minute, or other short time period), in order to protect servers from being overloaded and maintain high quality of service to many clients. In the case of Sikka APIs, your plan has an associated rate limit that is tracked and enforced over a second / minute window.

What does Sikka API rate limiting look like?

Sikka APIs use HTTP headers to communicate to your application what its rate limit is and how quickly it’s being used. Here’s an example of response header:

HTTP/1.1  200  OK
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
X-Rate-Limit-Limit: 5
X-Rate-Limit-Remaining: 3
X-Rate-Limit-Reset: 20
  • X-Rate-Limit-Limit indicates how many calls your application may make per time window. This time window is 60 seconds, but for the most reliable code you should use the X-Rate-Limit-Reset header.
  • X-Rate-Limit-Reset indicates when the current window ends, in seconds from the current time.
  • X-Rate-Limit-Remaining indicates how many calls you have remaining in this window.

So in this example, the application has 3 more calls remaining in the next 20 seconds; after that, the counter, X-Rate-Limit-Remaining, will reset back to 5. If the application were to make more calls than allowed, the API will return an HTTP 429 status code:

HTTP/1.1  429  Too  Many  Requests
X-Rate-Limit-Limit: 5
X-Rate-Limit-Remaining: 0
X-Rate-Limit-Reset: 10

What are some best practices for rate limits?

To make sure your application stays within rate limit always look headers in the API response and use headers to slow down API calls to Sikka API if needed.

For example the set of headers above — we’re allowed 5 calls in 60 seconds so ideally your application should have make 1 api call every 12 seconds. As in the example above , 3 more calls remaining over the next 20 seconds, so your application should make one call every 6 seconds so we get as close as possible to our rate limit without going over.

Divide the reset number by the remaining number to get the spacing we want between calls.

Notice that this approach doesn’t care that other clients may be consuming the same rate limit. For example, a high-volume application may want to have more than one server making queries to Sikka API. Since we recalculate the delay after each request, this approach tracks the correct rate limit even if multiple instances of the client are using the same API key. Hence, for distributed client applications, following these rate limit headers is the easiest way to ensure the application stays under the limits.

Handle 429 Even the best rate-limiting code is going to occasionally get an HTTP 429 error, so make sure your application handles them properly. In a back end application, this probably means slowing down (the example code above should handle this since X-Rate-Limit-Remaining is 0 on 429 responses) and trying again. In a front end application, this probably means showing a nice error message to the user.

Try to design the usage pattern that makes the fewest calls For example, let’s say your application wants to use Sikka API to show extra information about patients in your app. You could make the API call when rendering the patient page

Consider caching Along the same lines, even if your usage pattern demands that you make Sikka API calls at display time, if there’s significant overlap where the same person is displayed more than once, adding a cache between your application and Sikka API could be a big win. Consider using something like Memcache and refreshing data from the Sikka API only when it is older then data_insert_date of SPC

item.name: Modified Records

request.description:
'practice_events' api will return changed records in practice between given date range.

In response you will get data with flags (Deleted or Added or Updated). Response also contains changes array. Changes array represents field name , old value and new value.
example :

    "changes": 
             [
              {
               "field": "transaction_date",
               "old_value": "4/1/2016 12:00:00 AM",
               "new_value": "4/20/2016 12:00:00 AM"
               },
               {
               "field": transaction_entry_date",
               "old_value": "4/1/2016 12:00:00 AM",
               "new_value": "4/20/2016 12:00:00 AM"
               },
               {
                "field": "tooth_from",
                "old_value": "14",
                "new_value": "24"
                }
              ]
Parameters
request_key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

eventsRequired

Name of the events you want to get modified records. Events name must match with API name

loaded_startdateoptional

Start date for getting only changed record set. Date format : yyyy-mm-dd or yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss

loaded_enddateoptional

End date for getting only changed record set. Date format : yyyy-mm-dd or yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss

intervaloptional

Value must be integer.
'interval' parameter allows user to fetch records , which has been changed in last minutes.
This will be helpful when user does not know loaded_startdate/loaded_enddate and just want to fetch records which has been changed in last minutes (let say 5,10,15.. etc minutes). Maximun value for interval is 1440. You can fetch data which has been changed in last 24 hours (1440 minutes, interval=1440) without specifying loaded_startdate and loaded_enddate.
Note : Make sure Sikka platform utility is up and running on client machine. To check when Sikka platform utility (SPU) has refreshed, call 'authorized_practices' and get 'data_insert_date'.

status optional

Value should be current. This parameter will filter the result and return only the latest status of changed records without showing all previous changes.

API to find change logs of records :

For example :

  • To find patient’s modified records :
    https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/patients?loaded_startdate={}&loaded_enddate={}

  • To find appointments modified records :
    https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/appointments?loaded_startdate={}&loaded_enddate={}

  • To find transaction’s modified records :
    https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/transactions?loaded_startdate={}&loaded_enddate={}

In practice_events API

https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practice_events?loaded_startdate={}&loaded_enddate={}&events={name of data you want to retrieve }

For example , if you want to retrieve patients
https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practice_events?loaded_startdate={}&loaded_enddate={}&events=patients

Response :

{
 "offset": "1",
 "limit": "500",
 "total_count": "12",
 "execution_time": "13904",
 "pagination": 
  {
    "first": "[https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practice_events?offset=0&limit=1](https://localhost:44391/v4/practice_events?offset=0&limit=1)",
    "previous": "[https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practice_events?offset=0&limit=1](https://localhost:44391/v4/practice_events?offset=0&limit=1)",
    "current": "[https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practice_events?offset=1&limit=1](https://localhost:44391/v4/practice_events?offset=1&limit=1)",
    "next": "[https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practice_events?offset=2&limit=1](https://localhost:44391/v4/practice_events?offset=2&limit=1)",
    "last": "[https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/practice_events?offset=11&limit=1](https://localhost:44391/v4/practice_events?offset=11&limit=1)"
   },
  "items": [
            {
            ```response body same as events= api```
            ,"changes": []}]}
When to use practice_events API ?

If you want to get list of modified record using one API

Recommendations

  • Use it for short time range. like 24 hours or less.

  • Response will be slow and data accuracy will be affected if given date range is too big

  • Use either interval and flag or loaded_startdate, loaded_enddate and flag combination

  • If interval parameter is passed then loaded_startdate and loaded_enddate parameters will not be captured

Limitations :
Currently you will be able to get events from

  • patients

  • appointments

  • transactions

  • accounts_receivables

  • guarantors

  • guarantors_v2

  • practices

  • providers

  • treatment_plans

  • claims

  • claim_descriptions

  • prescriptions

  • reminders

  • lab_panels

We will update the list of events as we add in API

JSON is the default, and will be used if nothing is specified.

item.name: HTTP Compression
item.description:
Sikka API supports HTTP compression of response bodies using standards defined by the HTTP 1.1 specification. Enabling compression is recommended because it reduces bandwidth usage, and time spent retrieving data.

To enable compression, include the following HTTP header in the request:

GET /patients HTTP/1.1
Host: www.domain.com
Accept-Encoding: gzip,compress

The server understands the compression algorithms from Accept-Encoding and use algorithm to compress the representation before serving it. When successfully compressed, server lets know the client of encoding scheme by another HTTP header i.e. Content-Encoding

200  OK
Content-Encoding: gzip

Compression can save a lot of bandwidth, with very little cost in additional complexity. Also you may know that most web browsers automatically request compressed representations from website host servers – using above headers. References: https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec14.html#sec14.11 https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec14.html#sec14.3

item.name: Request Header
item.description:
✅ The request header supports 'request-key','app-id' & 'app-key' parameters that are common across all '/v4' endpoints; as a result, the header parameters aren’t documented with each endpoint.

List of request headers:

Request Header Description
request-key Request-Key header supports in all '/v4' APIs
app-id App-Id. Application id header supported in all 'v4' api or where it is required
app-key App-Key. Application Key header supported in all 'v4' api or where it is required

item.name: Response Header
item.description:
The headers contain a wealth of information that can help you track request and related information. HTTP Headers are an important part of the API request and response as they represent the meta-data associated with the API request and response. List of response headers:

Response Header Description
Data-Insert: <completed or in progress> 'data-insert‘ response header let API user know the status of data insert status. User should not fetch data when data insert is in progress. ‘data-insert’ header will help API users to know if data insert is in progress or not.
Data-Insert: completed - data insert completed, good to fetch data.
Data-Insert: in progress- data insert is in progress, wait till data insert completed
APIs has ‘data-insert’ header : transactions , patients , guarantors_v2 , patients_v2 , treatment_plans , appointments , patient_contract_roll_charges
Request-Key-Expires: <http-date> GMT If 'request_key' is active then 'Request-Key-Expires' value will be request_start_time + 24 hours, if 'request_key' is expired or inactive then request_end_time used as 'Request-Key-Expires'. http-date format (e.g. Wed, 21 Aug 2019 17:19:33 GMT)
'Request-Key-Expires' header will be available in all APIs where request_key is used.

item.name: Webhooks
Payload URL
item.description: This is your server endpoint that will receive the webhook payload. For example , you want to received events on https://<yourwebsitename>/api/receieve_payload Or https://<yourwebsitename>/api/payload

item.name: Content Type
item.description: At current Sikka Webhooks can be delivered using json format: The application/json content type will deliver the JSON payload directly as the body of the POST.

item.name: Events
item.description:
When configuring a webhook, you can choose which events you would like to receive payloads for. Only subscribing to the specific events you plan on handling is useful for limiting the number of HTTP requests to your server. You can change the list of subscribed events through the UI anytime. Each event corresponds to a certain set of actions that can happen to practice data or Sikka Platform Cloud. For example, if you subscribe to the Data Refresh event you'll receive detailed payloads every time when Sikka Practice Utility data refresh completed To create webhook , log in to Sikka API portal. Click on webhook. Enter required information and click on Add Webhook, it's time to check on your server to test the if events are receiving or not.

The available events are :

Name Description
Data_Refresh This indicates local SPC refresh is completed and data be available via API
patient When practice chang patient records , new patient added or deleted , this event will trigger.
transaction When practice chang transaction records , new transaction added or deleted , this event will trigger.
appointments When practice chang appointments records , new appointments added or deleted , this event will trigger.

Note : Sikka Practice Utility has to be up and running on server for all events

item.name: Payloads
item.description: Each event type has a specific payload format with the relevant event information. In addition to the fields documented for each event, webhook payloads include the office_id , practice_id on which the event occurred on

item.name: Headers
item.description:
HTTP POST payloads that are delivered to your webhook's configured URL endpoint will contain several headers:

Example delivery

POST /payload HTTP/1.1
Host: <host>
callback-key: xxxxxxxxx
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 6615
{
BODY
}

item.name: Sikka Webhook Retry Policies
item.description:
A retry policy is the process that will take over if/when one of your post back url requests fail .

When Do We Retry?

We will retry anytime we do not receive what we consider a successful response from the destination server. Below is a list of HTTP status codes that we consider as successes/failures.

Response Retry

  • 200 - success
  • 201 - success
  • 202 -success
  • 204 - success
  • 3xx - failure
  • 4xx - failure
  • 5xx - failure
  • Time out - failure
Attempt Webhook Notification will be delivered at
0 - This is the initial delivery attempt Immediately
1 - Delivery failed once, this is the second attempt failure attempt count =1
2 - Delivery failed twice, this is the third attempt failure attempt count = 2
3 - This is the third attempt failure attempt count = 3
4 - This is the fourth attempt failure attempt count = 4
5 - This is the fifth attempt failure attempt count = 5
After 5th failure attempt , webhook status will be inactive and will not send any notification

Webhooks allow you to set up your Apps which subscribe to certain events on practice data or Sikka Platform Cloud. When one of those events is triggered, we'll send a HTTP POST payload to the webhook's configured URL. Webhooks can be used to update your production server or CI builds and let you know about latest activity.

Once webhook configured , the webhook will be triggered each time one or more subscribed events occurs.

You can create up to 2 webhooks for each event on each target

Webhooks require a few configuration options before you use of them.

item.name: Resource Query Language (RQL)
item.description:
Data querying is a critical component of the Sikka APIs. With the advance of rich client-driven applications, new querying techniques are needed, and Resource Query Language (RQL) defines a very simple but extensible query language specifically designed to work within URIs and query for collections of resources.

Syntax

RQL is specifically designed for the web, using URI friendly delimiters, and using URI character encoding for all text/string values. Syntax makes it easy to use RQL with a variety of existing tools and processes. Please refer to the following sections our documentation for details on supported operators and syntax.

https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/?find==

Operators

Definition of the common operators supported by Sikka API

=eq_ → equal to

Filters for objects where api field values is equal to specified value. Example: 

GET /patients?find=firstname=eq_joe

The above example will filter all patients with first name equal to joe.

=neq_ → not equal to

Filters for objects where api field values is not equal to specified value. Example: 

GET /patients?find=firstname=neq_joe

The above example will filter all patients with first name not equal to joe.

=like_ → like

Filters for objects where api field values like to specified value.
Example: 

GET /patients?find=firstname=like_joe

The above example will filter all patients with first name starts with joe.

=gt → greater than ( supports integer & date format )

Filters for objects where api field values is greater than specified value.
Example: 

GET /patients?find=birthdate=gt_2017-01-01

The above example will filter all patients with birthdate is greater than 2017-01-01.

=gte → greater than equal to ( supports integer & date format )

Filters for objects where api field values is greater than equal to specified value.
Example: 

GET /patients?find=birthdate=gte_2017-01-01

The above example will filter all patients with birthdate is greater than and equal to 2017-01-01.

=lt → less than ( supports integer & date format )

Filters for objects where api field values is less than specified value.
Example: 

GET /patients?find=birthdate=lt_2017-01-01

The above example will filter all patients with birthdate is less than 2017-01-01.

=lte → greater than equal to ( supports integer & date format )

Filters for objects where api field values is less than equal to specified value.
Example: 

GET /patients?find=birthdate=lte_2017-01-01

The above example will filter all patients with birthdate is less than and equal to 2017-01-01.

::OR:: → Logical operators

Filters for objects based on logical operator and conditions
Example:
Find a patient with patient id = 79 OR guarantor id = 67 

GET /patients?find=patient_id=eq_79::OR::guarantor_id=eq_67

The above example will filter all patients with patient id 79 or guarantor id 67

::AND:: → Logical operators

Filters for objects based on logical operator and conditions
Example:
Find a patient with patient id = 79 OR firstname = joe 

GET /patients?find=patient_id=eq_79::AND::firstname=eq_joe

The above example will filter all patients with patient id 79 and first name 'joe'

item.name: Practice Query Language
item.description:
Benefits and Limitations

There are many benefits to using dynamic query (PQL) on API endpoints, for both the business and developers. However, there are limitations as well. In order to benefit from getting flexible schemas and result in API response, applications must share responsibility for managing the schema and the integrity of data

Benefits

  • Faster time to market: No learning curve (for developers) & Easy implementation.

  • Easier development: Build applications and features using simpler queries on Sikka APIs.

  • Less risk: Tightly integrated with licensing, so there will be no unauthorized access of data points

Limitations

  • Data models: Complex queries are not supported. Right now, you can join a maximum of 5 APIs in single PQL.

  • Limited API: Not all APIs are query able. Check API document for API list.

Use Cases

With dynamic query, APIs can support modern use cases for mission-critical applications, or simplify existing use cases, by combining the flexibility of dynamic query on APIs.

  • Review Management: Get list of patients visited to practice in one API call.

  • Finance: Get accounts receivable information of patients and guarantors.

  • IoT: Devices that need many possible information combinations can use PQL to get dynamic data.

  • Scheduling: Using PQL get the different combination of appointment scheduling and build app on top of that.

List of Sikka API support to PQL

You can use below Sikka APIs in PQL

  • practices

  • transactions

  • appointments

  • providers

  • patients

  • guarantors_v2

  • veterinary_patients

  • treatment_plans

  • claims

  • insurance_companies

  • accounts_receivables

  • accounts_receivables_by_patients

  • payment_plans

  • contact_details

  • products

  • optometry_product_details

  • medical_history

  • medical_notes

  • appointments_available_slots

  • outside_doctors

  • patient_status_history

  • patient_extended_info

  • patient_treatment_history

  • pms_users

  • multiple_referrals

  • preregistered_patients

  • staging_details

  • payment_types

  • procedure_codes

  • patient_contract_roll_charges

  • patient_contracts

  • appointment_statuses

  • guarantor_patient_mapping

  • patient_statuses

  • clinical_conditions

  • ins_company_details

  • patient_addresses

  • provider_time_clock

  • patient_charts

  • patient_conditions

  • treatment_plan_statuses

Architecture
Get data from different APIs

‘Practice_query’ (PQL) API allows you to run query on Sikka API and get result.
For example:
Get the list of patients (with patient & appointment details) who had appointment on ‘2017-05-30’.
If you use regular API then you have to call ‘patients’ and ‘appointments’ API. Using ‘practice_query’ (PQL), get data and merge it on client side

How to use ‘PQL’ in Sikka API testing tool

item.name: Onboarding practices to your application(s)
item.description:
✌ ARE YOU READY TO INTEGRATE WITH PRACTICES ?

You need to publish your application on Sikka marketplace

❶ Log into API portal >> Go to Publish Application, complete the process with required details.

❷ Go to Application(s) >> Select the application >> in "Application Details" section there is a "Marketplace Status", if it is "Published" then click on the link and it will redirect you to on boarding page.

❸ On board healthcare practice & install SPU

Download installation guide

❹ Start fetching and displaying data from healthcare practice

✅PREREQUISITE :

To fetch the data SPU (Sikka Practice Utility) must be installed on practice server.

item.name: Bulk Export
item.description:
Bulk export available for GOLD and upper API Plans.
Bulk export is an asynchronous service that can be used to export large amounts of data from Sikka cloud
to CSV and JSON . Its asynchronous nature allows for high speed
data export significantly reducing the time and resources consumed on the client side as compared to using APIs with pagination.

Check Bulk Export guide for more details

Limitation: Bulk Export does not support history data and authentication APIs

As we add more APIs, they will automatically documented here and available to you as per your API plan. For more information about a particular endpoint, click on its name. You will be taken to the API’s documentation page, which includes query parameters the API will accept, the JSON parameters will be in the response and an example.

❷ Select an APIs

request.description:

value should be true. it will return Credit Adjustment Types only.

value should be true. it will return Payment Types which require credit card details for POST transaction for Planet DDS PMS only.

request.description:
Key Performance Indicators

A key performance indicator (KPI) is a business metric used to evaluate factors that are crucial to the success of an organization.KPIs give you unique views of practice data that lead to action items to drive increased revenue and save valuable practice time.

Parameters

request_key required

Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

kpi_name optional

Kpi Name

There are over 300 key performance indicators, but below are available in v4 right now.

Table

Sr No Key Performance Indicators
1 accounts_receivable
2 active_patients
3 adjusted_collection
4 adjusted_production
5 adjusted_production_by_category
6 adjusted_production_by_dentist
7 adjusted_production_by_hygienist
8 adjusted_production_by_referral_source
9 adjusted_production_per_day
10 adult_prophy
11 all_hygienist_patient_visits
12 amount_by_transaction_type
13 average_daily_scheduled_production
14 collection_to_production
15 comprehensive_exams
16 hygiene_reappointment
17 new_patients
18 no_show_appointments
19 number_of_patients_seen
20 practice_report
21 provider_adjusted_production_per_hour
22 provider_total_production_per_hour
23 recall_potential_by_office_trend
24 recalls_by_office_trend
25 total_collection
26 total_patients
27 total_production
28 total_production_by_dentist
29 total_production_by_hygienist
30 total_production_by_provider_category
31 total_production_per_day
32 treatment_plan_analysis

request.description:
Returns staging details from practice

Parameters
request_key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

group_id optional

group id

detail_id optional

detail id

patient_id optional

Patient ID as per the management system

category optional

category

status optional

status

date_filter_onoptional

Field name on which stardate and enddate filter will be applied, valid values are: progress_date or estimated_surgery_date or stage_date(default)

startdate optional

format: yyyy-mm-dd, start date

enddate optional

request.description:
This API returns eligibility and benefit information. We are using third party API to get patient eligibility and benefit information so API response schema will be based on third API response. This API is not included in your API plan, for pricing contact your Sikka support manager.

Parameters

firstname Required

Patient first name. You can get patient first name using patients API.

lastname Required

Patient last name. You can get patient last name using patients API.

birthdate Required

You can get birthdate using patients API. Format should be YYYY-MM-DD

subscriber_id Required

You can get subscriber id using patients API. MetLife required patient SSN as subscriber id.

npi Required

You can get npi using providers API.

payer_id Required

You can get supported payer list using patient_eligibility_payers API.

is_dependent Optional

When requesting for a dependent this parameter value should be true.

dependent_firstname Optional

When requesting for a dependent this parameter value is required.

dependent_lastname Optional

When requesting for a dependent this parameter value is required.

dependent_birthdate Optional

When requesting for a dependent this parameter value is required.

dependent_subscriber_id Optional

When requesting for a dependent this parameter value is required.

dependent_group_numberOptional

When requesting for a dependent this parameter value is required.

is_clear_coverage Optional

to check enhanced eligibility pass this parameter. Value should be True/False. You can check clear coverage is supported or not using patient_eligibility_payers API.

request.description:
This API returns eligibility and benefit information. We are using third party API to get patient eligibility and benefit information so API response schema will be based on third API response. The currently supported test cases for sandbox are:

PRINCIPAL (full dental eligiblity and benefits response)
AETNA_DENTAL_PLANS (orthodontics flag set)
DD_CALIFORNIA (general dental eligibility and benefits response)
METLIFE (general dental eligibility and benefits response)

Parameters

firstname Required

Patient first name. You can get patient first name using patients API.

lastname Required

Patient last name. You can get patient last name using patients API.

birthdate Required

You can get birthdate using patients API. Format should be YYYY-MM-DD

subscriber_id Required

You can get subscriber id using patients API. MetLife required patient SSN as subscriber id.

npi Required

You can get npi using providers API.

payer_id Required

You can get supported payer list using patient_eligibility_payers API.

is_dependent Optional

When requesting for a dependent this parameter value should be true.

request.description:
The ‘data_sync_status’ api indicates if the practice management system integration is uptodate. ‘Status’ in api response should be equal to ‘refresh’ to make sure data you are receiving via api is correct.

The ‘data_sync_status’ api resource returns the real time status of practice management system connection.

"Status":"not_refresh" - Sikka integration is not able to connect practice management systems for more than 24 hours.

"Status":"refresh" - Sikka integration is connected practice management systems & data is up to date. Check data_insert_date for latest data fetch.

“Interval” parameter allows you to get the list of practices refreshed in the last 5, 10, 15… minutes.

Sample output

Status Office_id practice_name data_insert_date
refresh D1000 Pandya Clinics 2022-08-02T18:12:59
refresh D12435 Dholakpura Dental 2022-07-29T02:34:16
not refresh C10001 The Satyen's Dental Care 2021-06-09T23:31:07
Parameters
app-id Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

app-key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

office_id optional

Office_id you received from ‘authorized_practices’ API

interval optional (int)

Value must be integer.
'interval' parameter allows the user to get the list of practices which have been refreshed in the last minutes.

status optional

Status

practice_management_system optional

practice_management_system

practice_name optional

Practice name

SortBy
office_id default

Office_id you received from ‘authorized_practices’ API

practice_id

Practice ID

request.description:
POST ‘remote_command’ API allows to send command to 'Sikka Practice Utility' to refresh data and change refresh settings.

Headers
App-Id Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

App-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Request Body
office_id Required

Office_id you received from ‘authorized_practices’ API

spc_id Required

spc_id you received from health_check API.

command_name Required

command_name. Value should be refresh_data, change_setting_schedule_interval or change_setting_schedule_time.

interval_type Optional

interval_type is mandatory if command_name is change_setting_schedule_interval. Value should be Minutes or Hours.

interval_length Optional

interval_length is mandatory if command_name is change_setting_schedule_interval. if interval_type is Hours then value should be between 1 to 24. if interval_type is Minutes then value should be between 15 to 60.

new_value Optional

new_value is mandatory if command_name is change_setting_schedule_time. Value should be time in hh:mm tt format.

When you should use ‘health_check’ API ?

If you want to build SPC support portal and monitor SPC & ETL health.

Check SPC information. SPC should be active , there should be no error & refresh time should be current ( based on SPC schedule type setting )

In case of error or data is not updated , first check SPC_error_flag , spc_error_description

For more details you can check ETL information & PMS information ( start & end time , error description , notes , priority etc. )

For example, report SPC error codes and descriptions that will direct you to the cause. It may indicate that the PMS Version detected seems to be different from what is configured. Or it will tell you the last time we heard from that SPC, or what version it is, how much of the cpu is being used for the refresh, how much memory there is, the version of the OS, it’s IP addresses and more.

item.name: Overview, Purpose
item.description:
This API allows for secure initiation, authorization, and tracking of payment transactions through third-party processors. It supports real-time card and ACH payments, refunds, and transaction status inquiries while staying fully compliant with PCI-DSS and other financial regulations.

Note: These APIs do not change or write back to the practice management system. They work within Sikka’s secure financial gateway layer.

item.name: Security & Compliance
item.description:
Each endpoint is PCI-DSS compliant.

Card numbers and ACH routing are just a few examples of sensitive payment data that must never be sent in plaintext/query string.

The client and API need to talk over HTTPS (TLS 1.2+).

Authentication is done using a valid App-Key, App-Id, and/or Request-Key in each request.

item.name: Third-Party Processor Integration
item.description:
Sikka ONE APIs serve as a single gateway for multiple payment processors integrated together.

The actual processor being utilized is based on your setup at the practice level.

Processor-specific transaction IDs are provided in the API response, enabling developers to reconcile and confirm payments via the processor dashboard if necessary.

item.name: Error Handling
item.description:
Every error response contains an error code, error message, and payment provider message and code (if one exists, also refer Error Codes).

Retry transient network errors only, but do not retry declined transactions or bad credentials.

item.name: Refunds / Voids
item.description:
Refunds are required to cite the original transaction_id and go through the same third-party payment processor.

Full refunds are accommodated based on processor capability.

Void operations can only be done prior to settlement; otherwise, a refund should be provided.

item.name: Processor-Agnostic Design
item.description: Sikka Payment APIs allow smooth transition-onboarding of a new processor without client side code changes.

item.name: Note to Developers
item.description:
These endpoints can be used to run a real-time payment transactions via Sikka’s secure payment gateway. It will work with one or more third-party payment processors that you have configured with your end-user's account.

These APIs do not write back to the PMS - they are design for authorizing, capturing, and tracking payments.

Any sensitive data must be transmitted over HTTPS.

item.name: Merchant Onboarding Requirement
item.description:
Please note that you must have a valid merchant account setup and approved with your integrated payment processor before using this API.

If your practice or business has not yet gone through the onboarding process, feel free to reach out to Sikka Support to start the merchant onboarding process.

Once your account has been onboarded, you will receive processor credentials for your account, which will enable you to securely process payments through Sikka’s integrated payment processing gateway.

item.name: Charges Object
item.description:
The charges object represents additional transaction-related fees that may be included during a payment sale.

It helps record and track supplementary charges like taxes, tips, service fees, or shipping costs that accompany the primary payment.

Usage

This object can be used across all POST payment/sale endpoints:

  • payment/sale (Credit Card)

  • payment/sale (ACH)

  • payment/sale (Stored Card using billing_id)

Include it in the request payload under the "charges" property.

Notes

  • Charge names must be unique within the same transaction to avoid duplication.

  • Validation of data types and amounts is recommended before submitting the request.

  • The feature is compatible with all payment media types (credit_card, ach, billing_id).

"charges": [
        {
            "amount": "amount of the charge",
            "name": "name of the charge",
            "description": "description of the charge"
        },
        {
            "amount": "amount of the charge",
            "name": "name of the charge",
            "description": "description of the charge"
        }
    ]

item.name: Use cases
item.description:
💳 1. Card Verification Before Charging

Use Case:

Before processing any large or recurring payment, the client application verifies whether a customer’s credit card is valid and active.
Flow:
POST /payment/verify_card → verifies the card via temporary auth + void.
Outcome:

Ensures card validity without charging the customer.

🏦 2. Saving Cards for Future Transactions

Use Case:

A clinic or merchant wants to store a patient’s payment method securely for recurring billing or future visits.
Flow:
POST /payment/store_card → returns billing_id (tokenized card).
POST /payment/sale (with transaction_media: billing_id) → processes payment using stored card.
Outcome:

Simplifies future payments, reduces PCI exposure.

🧾 3. Manual Transaction Record Sync

Use Case:

Some payments may occur outside the integrated flow (e.g., processed via an in-clinic POS). The merchant wants to record such transactions in Sikka’s gateway for unified reporting.
Flow:
POST /payment/transactions → insert a transaction record for bookkeeping only (no real charge).
Outcome:

Keeps all transactions visible under one platform, regardless of source.

💰 4. Standard Credit Card Sale

Use Case:

A patient makes an online or in-office payment using a credit card.
Flow:
POST /payment/sale → with transaction_media: credit_card.

Optional charges field can include tax, tips, or other service fees.
Outcome:

Seamless one-step payment with itemized details.

🏦 5. ACH Transaction for Large Payments

Use Case:

A merchant wants to process large payments via ACH (bank-to-bank transfer) instead of credit cards.
Flow:
POST /payment/sale → with transaction_media: ach.
Outcome:

Lower fees and support for high-value transactions.

💵 6. Refund Process

Use Case:

A patient cancels a service and requests a refund.
Flow:
POST /payment/refund → for settled transactions.
POST /payment/refund2 → for standalone refunds to a card (requires processor configuration).
Outcome:

Supports flexible refund management for different scenarios.

🚫 7. Cancel an Unsettled Transaction

Use Case:

An incorrect transaction is identified before settlement.
Flow:
POST /payment/void → voids the transaction before funds are captured.
Outcome:

Prevents accidental or duplicate charges.

🧩 8. Fetch Historical Transaction Reports

Use Case:

An admin wants to generate a monthly report of all processed payments.
Flow:
GET /payment/transactions → returns up to 30 days of transactions (default) or filtered by date range.
Outcome:

Provides transaction visibility and reconciliation capabilities.

request.description:
This endpoint retrieves a list of transactions processed and stored within the Sikka Payment Gateway.

The API returns all transaction records matching the provided filter parameters and associated with the same App ID, Master ID, and API Host as the authenticated request context. This ensures isolation between applications and prevents cross-account data access.

If no date filters are provided, the API will automatically return transactions from the last 30 days.

Headers

Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
guarantor_id optional

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

patient_id optional

Patient ID as per the practice management system

provider_id optional

Provider ID as per the practice management system

practice_id optional

Practice ID of office

expiration_month optional

Card expiration month

expiration_year optional

Card expiration year

transaction_id optional

Unique ID of the transaction record

transaction_amount optional

Total amount involved in the transaction

reference_number optional

Internal or external transaction reference number

ticket_number optional

System-generated ticket number

description optional

description of transaction

cust_id optional

Cust ID (Customer ID)

transaction_date optional

Date when the transaction occurred

transaction_time optional

Time when the transaction occurred

email optional

Email id

city optional

City

state optional

State

zipcode optional

Zip code

office_id optional

Office ID

api_name optional

Name of the API

card_logo optional

Logo of the payment card

card_number_masked optional

Masked card number

transaction_type optional

Type of transaction

transaction_status optional

Status of the transaction

name optional

Name of customer

address1 optional

Primary address

address2 optional

Secondary address

phone optional

Phone number

transaction_media optional

Media used for the transaction

payment_provider optional

Name of the payment service provider

avs_code optional

Address Verification System response code from payment provider

order_id optional

generate order_id, format SPyyyyMMddhhmmssfffffff

authorization_code optional

Code provided by the payment gateway for authorization

payment_provider_message optional

Response message from the payment provider

response_code optional

response_code from the payment_provider

app_id optional

This is the ID assigned to the application when the application is registered on API portal

request_time optional

Timestamp when the API request was initiated.

chain_id optional

Chain ID

is_chain_head optional

Indicates if this record is the head of a transaction chain

sikkapay_reference_number optional

Reference number generated by Sikka for tracking

startdate optional

valid Start Date of the transaction, format yyyy-MM-dd

enddate optional

valid End Date of the transaction, format yyyy-MM-dd

Notes

  • Only transactions associated with the authenticated App ID, Master ID, and API Host will be returned.

  • By default, transactions from the last 30 days are retrieved if no date filters are specified.

  • Date fields should be passed in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) for best compatibility.

  • The API supports pagination and filtering for efficient querying in large datasets.

  • This endpoint is read-only and does not perform any modification to stored data.

  • Transaction data can include records added manually via the POST /payment/transactions endpoint or automatically through integrated processors.

request.description:
This endpoint is used to insert transaction records into the Sikka Payment Gateway database.

It is intended only for storing or recording transaction details, not for initiating or processing actual payments.

In some cases, transactions may occur through external or third-party processors or other integrated systems. This API provides a way to log and maintain those transaction records in a unified format within the Sikka Payment Gateway for reporting, reconciliation, and audit purposes.

The transaction data will be associated with the authenticated App ID, Master ID, and API Host.

Headers
Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
transaction_id Required

valid transaction_id of the transaction

transaction_date Required

valid date of the transaction, format yyyy-MM-dd

transaction_time Required

valid time of the transaction, format hh:mm:ss

transaction_type Required

type of the transaction, like, preauth, sale, store, verify, credit, refund

transaction_status Required

status of the transaction, like, approved, accepted, decline, declined, pending

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

name Required

Name of customer

order_id Required

generate order_id, format SPyyyyMMddhhmmssfffffff

payment_provider Required

Name of the payment service provider

response_code Required

response_code from the payment_provider

sikkapay_reference_number Required

Reference number generated by Sikka for tracking

transaction_amount Required

amount of the transaction, format without $ sign, xx.xx

transaction_media Required

Media used for the transaction

payment_provider_message optional

Response message from the payment provider

address1 optional

Primary address

address2 optional

Secondary address

city optional

City

state optional

State

zipcode optional

Zip code

email optional

Email ID

phone optional

Phone Number

card_number_masked optional

Masked card number

expiration_month optional

Card expiration month

expiration_year optional

Card expiration year

authorization_code optional

Code provided by the payment gateway for authorization

avs_code optional

Address Verification System response code from payment provider

description optional

description of transaction

reference_number optional

Internal or external transaction reference number

is_chain_head optional

Indicates if this record is the head of a transaction chain

chain_id optional

Chain ID

billing_id optional

billing_id, if available

Notes

  • This API does not trigger actual payment processing — it only records transaction details for internal tracking or reconciliation.

  • It can be used to log transactions that were processed externally or manually but still need to be reflected in Sikka Payment Gateway.

  • All transactions inserted through this endpoint can later be retrieved using the GET /payment/transactions endpoint.

  • The transaction record is always stored under the same App ID, Master ID, and API Host.

  • Ensure the merchant account is already onboarded with the third-party payment processor before recording related transactions.

request.description:
This endpoint verifies a credit card’s validity using a two-step process through the payment gateway.

Since not all payment providers provide a direct “card verification” API, this method uses an authorization-only transaction followed by an immediate void operation to confirm that the card details are valid and capable of successful authorization.

The endpoint does not charge the card — it simply performs a $0.01 authorization to ensure that the card is active, valid, and not blocked by the issuer.

Headers
Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
address1 optional

Primary address

card_number Required

Card Number

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

cvv optional

CVV

expiration_month Required

Card expiration month

expiration_year Required

Card expiration year

guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

name Required

Name of customer

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

zipcode optional

Zip code

payment_provider Required

Name of the payment service provider

Notes

  • This endpoint is used only for card verification, not for actual payment capture.

  • The transaction ID from the authorization step is used to perform the void request.

  • If the authorization fails, no void operation will be attempted.

  • API clients should store the verification status but not retain raw card details for PCI compliance.

  • All sensitive card data must be sent over HTTPS and handled in compliance with PCI-DSS standards.

request.description:
This endpoint securely stores a customer’s credit card details in the payment provider by creating a payment profile under the merchant account.

The API does not store or expose raw card details on Sikka servers. Instead, it returns a unique billing_id (token) that represents the stored card. This token can later be used for future transactions without re-entering card information, ensuring PCI-DSS compliance and enhanced user convenience.

Headers
Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
address1 optional

Primary address

address2 optional

Secondary address

card_number Required

Card Number

city optional

City

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

email optional

Email id

expiration_month Required

Card expiration month

expiration_year Required

Card expiration year

guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

name Required

Name of customer

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

phone optional

Phone number

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

state optional

State

zipcode optional

Zip code

payment_provider Required

Name of the payment service provider

Notes

  • Raw card details are never stored or logged on Sikka systems.

  • The returned billing_id acts as a secure token and can be used to initiate future transactions without re-entering card details.

  • Ensure compliance with PCI-DSS and tokenization standards when storing or reusing billing tokens.

  • If the same card is submitted again for the same customer, the API will attempt to detect duplicates and return the existing token if supported by the payment processor.

request.description:
This endpoint allows updating the stored credit card or its associated billing details in the payment provider system.

The billing_id (token of the stored card) is mandatory. Also refer Unmodifiable Fields at the end of this api.

All other fields are optional, and only the parameters provided in the request will be updated.

This ensures partial updates can be made safely without overwriting existing data.

Headers

Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
address1 optional

Primary address

address2 optional

Secondary address

city optional

City

state optional

State

phone optional

Phone number

email optional

Email id

expiration_month optional

Card expiration month

expiration_year optional

Card expiration year

card_number optional

Card Number

is_default optional

IsDefault true/false

zipcode optional

Zip code

billing_id Required

Unique billing identifier of the stored card record

Unmodifiable Fields

The following identifiers are mandatory at creation time but cannot be updated through this endpoint to maintain data integrity and linkage with customer and practice records:

Field Description
cust_id Customer ID linked to the patient.
guarantor_id Guarantor ID linked to the patient.
name Full name associated with the cardholder.
patient_id Patient ID linked to the card record.
practice_id Practice ID linked to the patient.
provider_id Provider ID linked to the patient.

request.description:
This endpoint deletes a stored credit card record in the payment provider system.

It allows removal of a previously saved card associated with a customer’s profile in the Sikka Payment Gateway.

The API performs a soft or permanent deletion (as per processor rules) and ensures that the deleted card token (billing_id) can no longer be used for any future transactions.

Headers
Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
cust_id Required

Cust ID (Customer ID)

guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

name Required

Name

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

zipcode optional

Zip code

payment_provider Required

Name of payment provider

billing_id Required

Unique billing identifier of the stored card record

Notes

  • This API does not delete any transaction records associated with the stored card; only the stored card profile is deleted.

  • Once deleted, the billing_id cannot be reactivated — a new card must be stored again using the POST payment/store_card endpoint.

  • The API ensures compliance with PCI-DSS by not storing sensitive card data directly.

request.description:
This endpoint initiates a sale transaction using ACH (Automated Clearing House) through the Sikka Payment Gateway.

It performs a direct debit transaction based on the provided ACH details and processes the payment through the connected payment processor.

Headers

Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
address1 optional

Primary address

address2 optional

Secondary address

city optional

City

email optional

Email id

phone optional

Phone number

state optional

State

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

name Required

Name of customer

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

transaction_amount Required

Total amount involved in the transaction

transaction_media Required

ach

zipcode optional

Zip code

routing_number Required

Routing Number

account_number Required

Account Number

account_type Required

Account Type

check_type Required

ppd/ccd/tel/arc/boc/web

check_number conditional

numeric up to 15 characters, needed if check_type=arc or boc

bank_name Required

name of the bank

payment_provider Required

Name of the payment service provider

Notes

  • This API supports only ACH-based transactions.

  • For credit card based transactions, refer to the POST payment/sale endpoint with "transaction_media": "credit_card".

  • Once processed, the transaction details will be recorded in the Sikka Payment Gateway for reporting and reconciliation.

  • Sensitive banking information is never stored in raw form and is handled in compliance with NACHA and PCI DSS guidelines.

request.description:
This endpoint processes a sale transaction using a credit card through the Sikka Payment Gateway.

It charges the customer’s credit card based on the provided card details and records the transaction in the payment gateway.

Headers
Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
address1 optional

Primary address

address2 optional

Secondary address

card_number Required

Card Number

city optional

City

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

cvv optional

CVV

email optional

Email id

Card expiration month

Card expiration year

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

Name of customer

Patient ID as per the practice management system

Phone number

Practice ID of office

state optional

State

Total amount involved in the transaction

transaction_media Required

Credit Card

zipcode optional

Zip code

payment_provider Required

Name of the payment service provider

Notes

  • transaction_media must always be "credit_card" for this endpoint to do transaction with credit card.

  • All sensitive card details are transmitted securely and handled per PCI DSS compliance.

  • Once processed, transaction details will be stored in the Sikka Payment Gateway for reconciliation and reporting.

  • For ACH-based transactions, refer to the POST payment/sale endpoint with "transaction_media": "ach".

	}]
}

request.description:
This endpoint processes a sale transaction using stored credit card details via a previously created billing_id (tokenized card).

The transaction is securely executed through the Sikka Payment Gateway using the token reference, without requiring raw card details in the request.

Headers

Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
address1 optional

Primary address

address2 optional

Secondary address

city optional

City

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

email optional

Email id

guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

name Required

Name of customer

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

phone optional

Phone number

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

state optional

State

transaction_amount Required

Total amount involved in the transaction

transaction_media Required

Credit Card

zipcode optional

Zip code

payment_provider Required

Name of the payment service provider

billing_id Required

Unique billing identifier of the stored card record

Notes

  • transaction_media must always be set to "billing_id" for this endpoint.

  • The billing_id must be valid and active — expired or deleted profiles will result in a failure.

  • No sensitive card details are required or transmitted in this call, ensuring PCI DSS compliance.

  • The processed transaction will be recorded in the Sikka Payment Gateway for future reference and reporting.

  • For direct card transactions, refer to the POST payment/sale endpoint with "transaction_media": "credit_card".

request.description:
The Refund API is used to process a refund for an existing, previously settled transaction in the Sikka Payment Gateway.

Refunds are supported only for settled transactions. Unsettled transactions (those still in authorization or batching state) cannot be refunded.

Key Rules

  1. Unsettled transactions cannot be refunded.
    Refund requests made before settlement will be rejected.

  2. Only settled transactions can be refunded.
    The original payment must have a completed settlement status.

  3. Partial refunds may be supported based on processor configuration.

  4. Refunds are processed against the original payment method used for the transaction.

Headers

Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

zipcode optional

Zip code

name Required

Name of customer

transaction_amount Required

Total amount involved in the transaction

original_transaction_id Required

original transaction id which is being refunded

order_id Required

generate order_id, format SPyyyyMMddhhmmssfffffff

payment_provider Required

Name of the payment service provider

Notes

  • Refund transactions will appear as separate entries in transaction reports with type refund.

  • Depending on the processor, settlement of refunds may take 1–3 business days.

  • Ensure that original_transaction_id and order_id belong to the same app context (App ID, Master ID, API Host).

request.description:
The Refund2 API is a standalone refund endpoint that allows issuing a refund directly to a provided credit card without referencing an existing settled transaction.

⚠️ Note: Additional configuration may be required on the payment processor side to enable standalone refunds.

Key Characteristics

  1. This endpoint does not require an existing transaction ID.

  2. The refund is issued directly to the provided credit card details.

  3. Supported only for credit card transactions.

  4. Processor configuration must support standalone credit refunds

Headers

Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

cust_id Required

CustId (Customer ID)

zipcode optional

Zip code

name Required

Name of customer

card_number Required

Card Number

expiration_month Required

Card expiration month

expiration_year Required

Card expiration year

transaction_amount Required

amount of the transaction, format without $ sign, xx.xx

payment_provider optional

Name of the payment service provider

Notes

  • This API is independent of prior sales and will not validate against previous transaction IDs.

  • Ensure the processor supports standalone refunds; otherwise, this request may fail.

  • Refunds will appear in reporting with transaction type refund2.

  • Settlement timelines may vary by processor (typically 1–3 business days).

request.description:
The Void API allows canceling an unsettled transaction before it has been captured or settled at the payment processor.

Key Characteristics

  1. Only unsettled transactions can be voided.

  2. Settled transactions cannot be voided — use the Refund API instead.

  3. The void operation cancels the original transaction entirely and prevents any charges from appearing on the customer’s statement.

Headers

Request-Key Required

A Valid 32 characters alpha-numeric request key

Parameters
guarantor_id Required

Guarantor ID as per the practice management system

patient_id Required

Patient ID as per the practice management system

provider_id Required

Provider ID as per the practice management system

practice_id Required

Practice ID of office

cust_id Required

Cust ID (Customer ID)

zipcode optional

Zip code

name Required

Name of customer

original_transaction_id Required

original transaction id which is being refunded

order_id Required

order_id of original_transaction_id

payment_provider optional

Name of the payment service provider

Notes

  • If the transaction has already settled, this API will return an error stating it cannot be voided.

  • For settled transactions, use POST payment/refund or POST payment/refund2.

  • Voided transactions are immediately marked as canceled and will not appear in settlement batches.

  • Typically used for same-day reversals or customer-initiated cancellations.
