item.name: GoHighLevel integration
item.description:
PMS - GoHighLevel Data Sync Configuration Documentation

📌 Overview

This section outlines the essential steps required to configure the integration between the Practice Management Software (PMS) and GoHighLevel (GHL) through Sikka Sync App. Follow each step carefully to ensure a smooth setup and data synchronization process.

GHL PMS Demo Integration

Step 1 : Set Up Workflows in GoHighLevel (GHL)

1.1 Configure Inbound and Outbound Workflows

  • Inbound Workflows:These are used to receive data from the PMS (such as new or
    updated patient and appointment information).

  • Outbound Workflows: These are used to send appointment updates and confirmations
    back to the PMS.

Please find the link to the GHL Snapshot for the GHL workflows (inbound and outbound). Import both workflows from this link to your sub-account, which handles the new data sync of patients and appointments.

GHL Workflow Snapshot

When setting up a new workflow, ensure all triggers,mappings and actions are properly defined.
The webhook URLs must be saved correctly to enable seamless data flow between systems.

Inbound Workflow in GHL

Outbound Workflow in GHL

Step 2 : Install the Sikka Platform Utility (SPU)

2.1 Install SPU

The Sikka Platform Utility (SPU), which facilitates the data sync process needs to be installed
where your PMS is hosted. A member of our CSO team can assist you with the SPU installation
process.

Step 3 : Configure the GHL Configurations in API Portal

3.1 Setup Inbound URL and API Keys

  • Navigate to the Sikka ‘s API portal
    (https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/portal/authentication/login)

  • Go to Settings -> GoHighLevel Configuration

  • Select your ‘Application’ and click on ‘Configure’ for your practice.
  • Enter the GHL field details configured in your GHL app workflow into this form.Follow the steps below to copy the Inbound URL and Location ID, and click ‘Save Configuration’ to complete the setup.

Copy the inbound URL from GHL inbound workflow.

Copy the location ID from the GHL app

Enter all the details gathered in the GHL Configuration form on the API Portal and click
on save.

Note: Operatory and Provider configured here become global defaults and will apply to every
write-back in this practice.

  • Map all the 6 GHL status to your PMS description(Example: "Cancelled" in GHL should correspond to "Broken" in PMS).Please note that based on the status that you map here,the system will use when posting appointment-status updates back to the PMS. Incorrect mappings can lead to mismatched updates.Please ‘Save Status Mappings’ before closing the modal.

Step 4 : Configure the Webhook URL in the API Portal

Please reach out to the Sikka Support team for the webhook URL and complete the configuration on your end.

Step 5 : Configure Calendar Availability in GHL (Optional)

1.1 Set Calendar Availability in GHL

  • Log in to GHL sub account and navigate to Calendars > Settings.

  • Set the working hours, time zones, and available appointment slots for each day of the week.

  • Make sure these match the actual office schedule to avoid conflicts or scheduling issues.

The current integration enables two-way data sync between the PMS and GoHighLevel (GHL).
New and updated patients and appointments are pushed from the PMS to GHL, while all new
and updated appointments are written back to the PMS.

By default, new appointments created in the PMS via writeback are assigned a default
status—‘Scheduled’ for OpenDental.

For Dentrix PMS, broken appointment writeback is currently not supported. This can be
implemented upon request if needed.

For rescheduled appointments in PMS, the update is posted in GHL by canceling the existing
appointment and creating a new one at the rescheduled time. When an appointment is
rescheduled in GHL, it cannot be written back to the PMS as GHL does not have a specific
status to identify rescheduled appointments.

Please create new patients in GHL or PMS with first name, last name and email as necessary
fields as we are using patients as unique combinations of first name, last name and email.

Support for new patient write‑back now exists, though it is subject to timing constraints due to the SPU’s refresh cycle.

⚠ Timing limitations in GoHighLevel

  • The SPU refreshes every 15 minutes, so if a new patient is created in GHL and an
    appointment is scheduled for them within the same 15-minute window (before the
    data sync) , the appointment write-back may not complete in time.

  • Likewise, if an appointment’s status is changed within 15 minutes of creation in GHL
    (before the data sync), that update may not be captured.
https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4

item.name: MCP
item.description:
Connect Sikka API to external tools using the Model Context Protocol

What is MCP?

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that lets Sikka API connect to external mcp and tools—like PowerBI MCP, Salesforce MCP or your internal APIs. Think of it as a universal connector for AI agents

Sikka AI API MCP Server, enabling AI agents to directly connect with Sikka’s comprehensive healthcare data platform.

The MCP Server bridges AI and real-world clinical data—giving intelligent agents secure access to patient records, billing data, insurance details, and practice management information across dental, veterinary, optometry, and chiropractic practices.

Why It Matters

With 50,000+ practice installations , Sikka.ai is the leading AI infrastructure provider for retail healthcare.

Until now, connecting AI agents to the Sikka ONE API required custom development and complex documentation.

The AI-API MCP Server eliminates that friction—providing plug-and-play connectivity, natural language access, and built-in guidance for standard healthcare workflows.

How It Works

The MCP Server acts as an intelligent wrapper around the Sikka ONE API.

Your AI agent sends a single natural language prompt, and the server translates it into the appropriate REST API calls.

Supported Workflows

  • Retrieve patient demographics or treatment history

  • Check appointment availability and open slots

  • Verify insurance eligibility and balances

  • Fetch transactions, production, or KPIs for business analytics

Core Features

✅ Natural Language Understanding – No manual endpoint mapping

✅ Contextual Awareness – Understands patient, provider, and date context

✅ Unified Access – Works across all Sikka-connected practice management systems

🔌 How to Connect

The MCP server is part of Sikka’s secure healthcare infrastructure, compliant with HIPAA and PHI handling standards.

Endpoint

POST https://api.sikkasoft.com/v4/mcp/query
headers = {
  'Request-Key': 'your_practice_request_key',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}

🔑 Replace your_practice_request_key with the actual request key generated from POST request_key

💬 Example Queries

Query What it Does
“Show all appointments for patient Sonia.” Retrieves all appointments linked to Sonia.
“Verify insurance coverage for John Doe.” Checks payer eligibility for the patient.
“Get billing history for patient ID 12345.” Returns payment transactions.
“Show me confirmed appointments for tomorrow.” Lists next-day appointments by provider.
“What are the KPIs for January 2025?” Fetches production and financial metrics.
"find me open slots for appointments" Return all open appointment slots based on existing appointments , providers and operatory schedules.

StartFragment

🚀 When to Use the MCP Server

The MCP server is ideal for real-time AI workflows and intelligent healthcare agents:

Use Cases

  • Patient Care Coordination – Unified patient view across visits and providers

  • Revenue Cycle Automation – Automate claims, payments, and follow-ups

  • Practice Intelligence – Generate instant insights with KPI queries

For bulk analytics or ETL workflows, use the direct Sikka ONE API endpoints.

For no-code app building, explore SAI ( Sikka Artificial Intelligence ).

⚡ Performance and Scale

Built on the same infrastructure serving tens of thousands of practices, Sikka MCP delivers:

  • 99.9% uptime SLA

  • Enterprise-grade security

  • Healthcare-specific query optimization

  • Low-latency, real-time responses
